title: refactor(chat): render the composer agent menu via chat-ui AgentPicker
body: ## Summary Third slice of ECA-1288: builds a new `AgentPicker` in `@zooclaw/chat-ui` (composing `@zooclaw/design-system` primitives), wires `UnifiedChatComposer` to it, and **deletes the app-local `ComposerAgentMenu`**. Both New Chat and session chat render through the shared composer, so this lands on both. Net **~-200 lines** (206 insertions / 414 deletions across tracked files, plus the new `AgentPicker` + app avatar adapter). ### The avatar crossing (the interesting bit) The agent avatar can be an image, an emoji, or an **animal** — and the animal case needs the app's `AnimalAvatar` (an onboarding component). So the avatar crosses into the shared layer **as a slot, not as data**: - The app's `resolveComposerAgentPresentation` (unchanged) normalizes each agent into `{ displayName, description, avatar-union }`. - A thin **app-side** `ComposerAgentAvatar` adapter renders the union to a `ReactNode` (`image` -> ``, `animal` -> `AnimalAvatar`, `emoji` -> ``). - `AgentPickerOption` carries `avatar: ReactNode`; chat-ui's `AgentPicker` just places it. So **chat-ui never imports `AnimalAvatar`**, and the public view-model carries no domain types (a `ReactNode` is not a d
